Hi, I do not think we have met. I am Lucimara. What brings you here tonight?
Nice to meet you, Lucimara. I am Mark. I am a buyer. I source ingredients for a cosmetics brand back in the States. And you?
Oh, that is common ground, then. I am on the other side of that. I run a company that makes the ingredients buyers like you look for.
No way, that is perfect timing. What kind of ingredients does your company make?
We are a company that produces chemical ingredients which go into shampoos, creams, and lotions.
That is exactly what I am here to find. Is this your first time at the Lyon fair?
It is, and I love it already. Lyon is a city where the food is incredible. Have you tried it yet?
Not yet, but everyone keeps telling me the same thing. Look, I would really like to continue this. Do you have a card?
Of course, here you go. It was great meeting you, Mark. Let us stay in touch. I am going to mingle a little, but let us talk soon.
Likewise. I will email you next week. Enjoy the reception, Lucimara!

Welcome to your first big trade fair. Let me give you one piece of advice that took me years to learn. The people who do well here are not the ones with the best products. They are the ones who are easiest to talk to. So when you walk into the reception tonight, do not rush to sell anything. Find some common ground first. Ask a simple question that opens the door, something like, what brings you here, or, have you been to this fair before. Those are the icebreakers that make a stranger relax. And then listen, really listen, because a good follow-up question is worth more than a business card. When you meet someone whose company does something close to yours, that is the person you want to catch up with later. Keep it lighthearted, keep it short, and when it is time to move on, just say, it was great meeting you, let us stay in touch. That is how you wrap up a conversation without being rude. Then you mingle, and you do it again. That is the whole game.

Hi Lucimara, it is Camille from the Lyon office. I just wanted to give you a quick heads up about the reception tonight, because I know it is your first one. It starts at seven, in the main hall, which is the big room where they had the opening ceremony this morning. Do not worry about knowing anyone. Nobody does at first, and that is the whole point of the evening. There is a woman named Sofia who runs the cosmetics pavilion. She is the person who I told you about on the phone, and she is really easy to talk to, so I will introduce you. My one tip is to bring plenty of cards, because you will exchange them all night. And if the small talk ever feels hard, just ask people about the city. Lyon is a place where everyone has an opinion about food, and that always breaks the ice. See you at seven. Look for me near the entrance.
